items.question_text: Under 9-5-1, which military aircraft may receive vectoring service for jettisoning external stores at any time?
items.explanation: Per 9-5-1a, USAF, ANG, and Air Force Reserve aircraft may receive vectoring service at any time. Civil operators require a written agreement (9-5-1b).

choices.text: USAF, ANG, and Air Force Reserve aircraft.
All military aircraft regardless of service branch.
Only USAF active-duty aircraft; ANG and Reserve require a written agreement.
Navy and Marine Corps aircraft operating from land bases.

items.question_text: Under 9-5-1, why do civil operators and civil aircraft companies need a written agreement to receive vectoring service for jettisoning external stores?
items.explanation: Per Note 2, 'Any similar vectoring service given to civil operators and civil aircraft companies operating Air Force type aircraft requires written agreement between the FAA and the user to relieve the FAA of possible liability. The regional counsel's office acts for FAA in executing this agreement.'

choices.text: To ensure the FAA receives payment for the vectoring service.
To relieve the FAA of possible liability, with the regional counsel's office acting for FAA.
To certify that the pilot is qualified to jettison stores under FAA supervision.
To document the specific drop area coordinates for NOTAM publication.
